title: 'Burnham's power play' and Widdecombe 'hit 21 times with a hammer'
description: The papers are dominated by reports about the former MP's death and the prime minister's first full day in office.
content: The papers continue to focus on Andy Burnham's start as prime minister. "Who is going to pay for Andy's largesse?" asks the Daily Mail, external on its front page. It says the Conservatives have questioned where the money will come from for what the ... [1724 chars]

title: UK humanitarian worker monitored for Ebola in London hospital
description: The health worker was evacuated to the UK from the DR Congo after being potentially exposed to the virus.
content:
A humanitarian worker in the Democratic Republic of Congo has been medically evacuated to the UK after a potential healthcare‑related exposure to Ebola.
The UK Health Security Agency (UKHSA) said the individual, who is a UK resident and has not been ... [1280 chars]

title: Sharon remembers Ozzy Osbourne one year on from his death
description: Sharon Osbourne shares with the BBC her memories and hopes for the future.
content:
Sharon Osbourne, the widow of heavy metal pioneer Ozzy, has used the first anniversary of his death to tell fans to remember him by playing music "really loud".

title: More women in their 20s are getting type 2 diabetes
description: England is seeing "a worrying increase" in women in their 20s developing type 2 diabetes, say experts. Here's what to look out for.
content:
England is seeing a "worrying increase" in young women in their 20s developing type 2 diabetes, experts at Imperial College London have warned.

title: Great Apes kiss and cuddle to keep the peace, says study
description: Hugging before stressful events may be an ancient way of building trust, say scientists studying apes.
content:
Touching, embracing and kissing before stressful events may be an ancient way of building trust, according to scientists studying the behaviour of our closest ape relatives.
